questionText:
With reference to NITI Aayog, consider the following statements:
1. It was established by an executive resolution of the Government of India.
2. It is chaired by the Prime Minister of India.
3. Its Governing Council includes Chief Ministers of all the States and Union Territories with Legislatures.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
On January 1, 2015, the NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India) was established as the successor to the Planning Commission.
It was established by an executive resolution of the Government of India (i.e., Union Cabinet). Hence, it is also neither a Constitutional body nor a Statutory body. In other words, it is a non-constitutional or extra-constitutional body (i.e., not created by the Constitution) and a non-statutory body (not created by an Act of Parliament). So, statement 1 is correct.
The Prime Minister of India is the Chairperson of NITI Aayog. The Vice-Chairperson of NITI Aayog is appointed by the Prime Minister. So, statement 2 is correct.
The Governing Council comprises the Chief Ministers of all the States, Chief Ministers of Union Territories with Legislatures (i.e., Delhi, Puducherry and Jammu and Kashmir) and Lt. Governors of other Union Territories. So, statement 3 is correct.
The meetings of the Governing Council have been held under the chairmanship of the Prime Minister with Chief Ministers/Lt Governors of the States/UTs and other members of the Governing Council.
Regional Councils of NITI Aayog are formed to address specific issues and contingencies impacting more than one state or region. These are formed for a specified tenure. These are convened by the Prime Minister and comprise the Chief Ministers of States and Lieutenant Governors of Union Territories in the region. These are chaired by the Chairperson of the NITI Aayog or his nominee.
So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Polity

questionText:
Consider the following Statements:
1. Industrial licensing was abolished for industries like alcohol and cigarettes
2. Rupee was devalued against foreign currencies
3. Foreign investment limit in banks was raised
4. Increase of tariff rates
How many of the above financial reforms were introduced in India after the 1991 economic crisis?
explanation:
In 1991, India met with an economic crisis relating to its external debt — the government was not able to make repayments on its borrowings from abroad; foreign exchange reserves dropped to levels that were not sufficient for even a fortnight. In this scenario, India announced the New Economic Policy (NEP) which consisted of wide-ranging economic reforms.
Industrial licensing was abolished for almost all product categories except for industries like alcohol, cigarettes, hazardous chemicals, industrial explosives, electronics, aerospace and drugs and pharmaceuticals. The only industries which are now reserved for the public sector are a part of atomic energy generation and some core activities in railway transport. So, point 1 is correct.
In 1991, as an immediate measure to resolve the balance of payments crisis, the rupee was devalued against foreign currencies. This led to an increase in the inflow of foreign exchange. So, point 2 is correct.
The reform policies led to the establishment of private sector banks, Indian as well as foreign. The foreign investment limit in banks was raised to around 74 per cent. Those banks which fulfil certain conditions have been given the freedom to set up new branches without the approval of the RBI. So, point 3 is correct.
Other trade policy reforms aimed at (i) dismantling of quantitative restrictions on imports and exports (ii) reduction of tariff rates and (iii) removal of licensing procedures for imports. So, point 4 is not correct.
So, only three of the above financial reforms were introduced in India after the 1991 economic crisis.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Sl. No. Five-Year Plan Major Event
1. Third Plan : Nationalisation of banks
2. Fourth Plan : Introduction of Green Revolution Programme
3. Ninth Plan : Second Nuclear Test
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
explanation:
During the third Five Years Plan (1961-66), the priorities were on self-sufficiency in food grains, meeting the raw material needs of industries and increasing exports. During this period, the Green Revolution programme was started on a small scale. So, pair 1 is not correctly matched.
Fourth Plan (1969–74) was based on the Gadgil strategy with a special focus on the ideas of growth with stability and progress towards self-reliance. The nationalization of 19 banks and greater control and regulation of the economy were some of the salient features of this plan. So, pair 2 is not correctly matched.
Ninth Plan (1997–2002) emphasized on the seven identified Basic Minimum Services (BMS). The issue of fiscal consolidation became a top priority of the government for the first time during this plan. The Second Nuclear Test under ‘Operation Shakti’ was conducted in Pokhran in 1998 during this plan. So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
So, only one of the above pairs is correctly matched.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: ‘Export Preparedness Index’ is launched by which one of the following?
options: c) Ministry of Commerce and Industry
explanation:
The Export Preparedness Index (EPI) is launched by the NITI Ayog. For the Indian context, the EPI extends India’s innate heterogeneity to its exports and serves as a tool to help States develop strategies for their context-specific challenges.
It provides a comprehensive analysis of the country’s export trends, district-level performance, and insights from the analysis of states and UTs.
The structure of the EPI includes 4 pillars: Policy; Business Ecosystem; Export Ecosystem; and Export Performance.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the ‘India Infrastructure Project Development Fund Scheme (IIPDF)’, consider the following statements:
1. It is launched by the Department of Economic Affairs (DEA), Ministry of Finance.
2. It provides financial incentives to Public-Private Partnership (PPP) projects under both the Central and State Governments.
3. Funding under the IIPDF Scheme is in addition to the Viability Gas Funding (VGF) Scheme.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
India Infrastructure Project Development Fund (IIPDF) Scheme is launched by the Department of Economic Affairs (DEA), Ministry of Finance. It is a central sector scheme for financial support for project development expenses of Public Private Partnership (PPP) Projects. So, statement 1 is correct.
The IIPDF will aid the development of quality Public-Private Partnership (PPP) projects by providing necessary funding support to the project sponsoring authorities, both in the Central and State Governments, for creating a shelf of bankable viable PPP projects for achieving the vision of modern infrastructure for the country. So, statement 2 is correct.
India Infrastructure Project Development Fund (IIPDF) was created in the Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry of Finance in 2007-08 to support the development of Public Private Partnership (PPP) projects that can be offered to the private sector.
Funding under the IIPDF Scheme is in addition to the Viability Gas Funding (VGF) Scheme, through which infrastructure projects undertaken through PPP mode that are economically justified but commercially unviable are supported. So, statement 3 is correct.
So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
Knowledge Box
The IIPDF scheme will provide necessary financial support to the project sponsoring authorities for creating a shelf of bankable viable PPP projects for achieving the vision of modern infrastructure for the country.
The IIPDF will contribute up to 75% of the project development expenses to the sponsoring authority as an interest-free loan. The balance of 25% will be co-funded by the sponsoring authority. On successful completion of the bidding process, the project development expenditure would be recovered from the successful bidder. However, in the case of failure of the bid, the loan would be converted into a grant. In case the Sponsoring Authority does not conclude the bidding process for some reason, the entire amount contributed would be refunded to the IIPDF.
subject: Economics

questionText: The Strengthening Multimodal and Integrated Logistics Ecosystem (SMILE) program is related to the provisions of lending by which one of the following?
explanation:
The Asian Development Bank (ADB) has approved a $250 million policy-based loan to India for reforms aimed at strengthening and modernising logistics infrastructure, improving efficiency and lowering costs. It will finance the first subprogram of the Strengthening Multimodal and Integrated Logistics Ecosystem Program, which supports the government’s efforts to create a comprehensive policy, planning, and institutional framework.
The Strengthening Multimodal and Integrated Logistics Ecosystem (SMILE) is a program of ADB. It is a programmatic policy-based loan to support the government for undertaking wide ranging reforms in the logistics sector in India. The program aims to strengthen the institutional and policy framework for inter-ministerial coordination and planning for incentivizing private sector participation in multimodal logistics projects (MMLPs), improving efficiencies in external trade logistics and encouraging the use of smart and automated systems for improved service delivery.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Which of the following constitute the Aggregate Technical & Commercial Losses in the context of Power Distribution Companies (DISCOMs) in India?
1. Inefficiency in bill collection
2. Default in payments made
3. Magnetic losses in transformers
4. Energy dissipated in the conductors
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation:
The Aggregate Technical & Commercial Loss (AT & C Loss) is the difference between the amount of electricity received by a distribution company from the transmission company and the amount of electricity for which it invoices its customers plus the loss of the adjusted collection.
It provides a realistic picture of the loss borne by the power distribution companies. It is a combination of energy loss (Technical loss + Theft + inefficiency in billing) & commercial loss (Default in payment + inefficiency in collection). So, points 1 and 2 are correct.
The technical losses are due to energy dissipated in the conductors, equipment used for transmission lines, transformers, sub-transmission line and distribution line and magnetic losses in transformers. So, points 3 and 4 are correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the ports in India:
1. Visakhapatnam Port in Andhra Pradesh is a land-locked harbour.
2. All coastal States have at least one major port except Goa and Karnataka.
3. All Major ports are under the control of the Central Government.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
Visakhapatnam Port in Andhra Pradesh is a land-locked harbour, connected to the sea by a channel cut through solid rock and sand. An outer harbour has been developed for handling iron-ore, petroleum and general cargo. So, statement 1 is correct.
The coastal States of India are Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha and West Bengal. Each of these Coastal States have a Major Port. So, statement 2 is not correct.
Gujarat: Deendayal Port Trust (Kandla)
Maharashtra: Jawaharlal Nehru Port Trust, Mumbai Port
Goa: Mormugao Port Trust
Karnataka: New Mangalore Port Trust
Kerala: Cochin Port Trust
Tamil Nadu: V. O. Chidambaranar Port Trust (formerly: Tuticorin Port), Chennai Port, Kamarajar Port Trust (formerly: Ennore Port)
Andhra Pradesh: Visakhapatnam Port Trust
Odisha: Paradip Port Trust
West Bengal: Kolkata Port Trust
Any port under the control of the Central Government through the Union Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways is known as a Major Port of India. The intermediate and minor ports are under the control of their respective State governments. So, statement 3 is correct.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following:
1. National Securities Depository Limited (NDSL)
2. Multi Commodity Exchange Clearing Corporation
3. Bombay Stock Exchange
4. Metropolitan Stock Exchange of India
How many of the above institutions are/are covered under the category of Market Infrastructure Institutions (MII)?
explanation:
A Market Infrastructure Institution (MII) is a financial entity that provides essential infrastructure for running the daily operations in the Stock Market/Capital Markets.
Stock exchanges, depositories and clearing houses are all Market Infrastructure Institutions (MIIs) and constitute a key part of the nation's vital economic infrastructure.
There are two depositories — charged with the safekeeping of securities and enabling their trading and transfer — that are tagged MIIs: the Central Depository Services Limited (CDSL) and the National Securities Depository Limited (NDSL). So, point 1 is correct.
The regulator also lists seven clearing houses including the Clearing Corporation of India Limited and Multi Commodity Exchange Clearing Corporation. Clearing houses, for their part, help validate and finalise securities trades and ensure that both buyers and sellers honour their obligations. So, point 2 is correct.
Among stock exchanges, the SEBI lists seven, including the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE), the National Stock Exchange (NSE), the Multi Commodity Exchange of India and the Metropolitan Stock Exchange of India. So, points 3 and 4 are correct.
So, all four of the above institutions are/are covered under the category of Market Infrastructure Institutions (MII).
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: The ‘Build Ahead Coalition’ is related to:
options: a) development of blockchain solutions for public sector services
b) sustainable manufacturing of construction materials
d) development of charging infrastructure for Electric Vehicles
explanation:
Build Ahead Coalition is an industry-first coalition consisting of forward-leaning businesses committed to scaling decarbonisation efforts across the Indian construction value chain.
The coalition members include Godrej Construction, JLL, JSW Cement, Lodha, SED Fund and Shell India.
Using collective action and collaboration, Build Ahead aims to support India's pledge to achieve net-zero emissions by 2070 by increasing the adoption of low-carbon building materials in construction, and reducing emissions during the design, construction, use and end-of-life phases of real estate, construction and infrastructure projects.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements regarding the Open Acreage Licensing Policy (OALP):
1. It was launched under the ambit of Hydrocarbon Exploration and Licensing Policy (HELP).
2. It allows the investors to bid for blocks of their choice throughout the year.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
The Open Acreage Licensing Policy (OALP) was launched under the ambit of Hydrocarbon Exploration and Licensing Policy (HELP). The New Exploration Licensing Policy (NELP) was the predecessor of HELP. So, statement 1 is correct.
HELP was launched with the clear objective of boosting the production of oil & gas in the Indian sedimentary basin and reducing hydrocarbon import dependency by 10% by 2022.
The OALP mechanism allows the investors to carve out blocks of their choice by assessing exploration & production data available at National Data Repository (NDR) & by submitting an Expression of Interest (EoI). It can be submitted throughout the year without waiting for a formal bid round from the government. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: It is a form of cloud computing that delivers fundamental computing, network and storage resources to consumers on-demand, over the Internet, and on a pay-as-you-go basis. It enables consumers to reduce the maintenance of on-premises data centres and save money on hardware costs. This is the most likely description of:
explanation:
Infrastructure as a Service is a form of cloud computing that delivers fundamental computing, network, and storage resources to consumers on-demand, over the Internet, and on a pay-as-you-go basis. It enables end users to scale and shrink resources as per the requirement by reducing the need for high, up-front capital expenditures or unnecessary “owned” infrastructure. It emerged as a popular computing model in the early 2010s, and since that time, it has become the standard abstraction model for many types of workloads.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
Knowledge Box
Desktop as a service is a cloud computing offering in which a third party hosts the back end of a virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI) deployment. In Desktop as a service, desktop operating systems run inside virtual machines on servers in a cloud provider's data centre. The entire necessary support infrastructure, including storage and network resources, also lives in the cloud. Desktop as a service provider streams virtual desktops over a network to a customer's endpoint devices, where end users may access them through client software or a web browser.
Platform-as-a-Service is a cloud computing model that provides customers a complete cloud platform of hardware, software, and infrastructure for developing, running, and managing applications without the cost, complexity, and inflexibility that often comes with building and maintaining that platform on-premises.
Software as a service is a cloud-based software delivery model in which the cloud provider develops and maintains cloud application software which provides automatic software updates and makes software available to its customers via the internet on a pay-as-you-go basis. The first generation of Software as service solutions was evolved, inflexible, and designed to solve a single business problem in the early 2000s.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the landlord port model, consider the following statements:
1. In this model, the publicly governed port authority acts as a regulatory body.
2. The private entities that carry out port operations own all available fixed and mobile assets.
3. The landlord port gets a share of the revenue from the private entity.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
In the landlord port model, the publicly governed port authority acts as a regulatory body and as a landlord while private companies carry out port operations—mainly cargo-handling activities. So, statement 1 is correct.
The port authority owns the land and all available assets—fixed and mobile—and performs all regulatory and port functions. Here, the port trust is both the landlord and the cargo terminal operator. So, statement 2 is not correct.
In the landlord port model, infrastructure is leased to private firms that provide and maintain their own superstructure and install their own equipment to handle cargo. In return, the landlord port gets a share of the revenue from the private entity. So, statement 3 is correct.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the Nice Classification (NCL), consider the following statements:
1. It is developed by the World Health Organisation (WHO) to classify diseases internationally.
2. This classification applies in countries that are not party to the Nice Agreement, 1957.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
The Nice Classification (NCL) is an international classification of goods and services applied for the registration of trademarks and service marks. It was established by the Nice Agreement (1957). So, statement 1 is not correct.
The countries party to the Nice Agreement constitute a Special Union within the framework of the Paris Union for the Protection of Industrial Property. Each of these countries is obliged to apply the nice classification either as the principal classification or as a subsidiary classification.
Use of this classification is mandatory for both the national and international registration of marks effected by the International Bureau of the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), under the protocol relating to the Madrid Agreement (1981) concerning the International Registration of Marks, and for the registration of marks by the African Intellectual Property Organization (OAPI), the Benelux Trademark Office and Office for Harmonization in the Internal Market (Trade Marks and Designs) (OHIM). The Nice Classification is also applied in a number of countries which are not party to the Nice Agreement. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited (CIBIL), consider the following statements:
1. It calculates and maintains the credit history of both individuals and business corporations.
2. It takes into account credit profiles of the borrowers for the previous five years.
3. The decision to lend money is solely dependent on the CIBIL.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited (CIBIL) is the most popular of the four credit information companies licensed by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). The other three are Experian, Equifax and Highmark.
CIBIL score or credit score is the reflection of a person’s credit history and is the first check banks or any other financial institutions do to check the credibility of the borrower.
It maintains credit files of both individuals and business corporations. So, statement 1 is correct.
A CIBIL score takes time to build up and usually it takes between 18 months to 36 months or more of credit usage to obtain a satisfactory credit score. So, statement 2 is not correct.
It consists of a three-digit numeric summary of the borrower's credit history. The value may range between 300 to 900. An excellent CIBIL score comes in the range of 750-900 and the one coming in the range of 300-500 comes under the poor category.
The CIBIL score works as a first impression for the lender. The higher the score, the better your chances of the loan being reviewed and approved. The decision to lend is solely dependent on the bank and CIBIL does not in any manner decide if the loan/credit card should be sanctioned or not. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: It is a new industry that is expanding rapidly and is expected to be increasingly important in the future. It is characterised by high growth rates and numerous start-up establishments with an abundance of venture capital investments. This is the most likely description of:
explanation:
A sunrise industry is a new industry that is expanding rapidly (e.g., the telecom industry in the mid-1990s) and is expected to be increasingly important in the future. The term was coined in 1983 to describe the then, successful manufacturing industries, upon which the economic sun was supposedly rising. This is also used as an antonym for an earlier term ''sunset industry'' which is an industry that has existed for a long time and that is less successful and making less profit than previously.
Sunrise industry is an inherent concept that shows hope of a rapid boom for a developing sector or market in its infancy stage. Typically, the sunrise industries are characterized by high growth rates, numerous start-ups, and a wealth of venture capital funding. Such industries create a lot of "buzz" as investors take an interest in their long-term growth prospects and raise public awareness.
As a sunrise industry develops, it may transition to the maturity stage and then to the sunset stage. To remain relevant and on an upward trajectory, sunrise industries must prove their viability and sustainability.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Index of Eight Core Industries:
1. It is released by the Office of Economic Adviser, Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT).
2. Electricity has the highest weightage in the Index.
3. It comprises more than 75% of the weight of items included in the Index of Industrial Production (IIP).
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
The Eight Core sectors include - Coal, Crude Oil, Natural Gas, Refinery Products, Fertilizers, Steel, Cement and Electricity.
The Index of Eight Core Industries is released by the Office of Economic Adviser, Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT). The Index of Industrial Production (IIP) is released by the National Statistics Office (NSO). So, statement 1 is correct.
The eight core sector industries in decreasing order of their weightage: Refinery Products (weight: 28.04 per cent)> Electricity (weight: 19.85 per cent)> Steel> Coal> Crude Oil> Natural Gas> Cement> Fertilisers. So, statement 2 is not correct.
The Eight Core Industries comprise only 40.27 per cent of the weight of items included in the Index of Industrial Production (IIP). It is currently calculated using 2011-2012 as the base year. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
Knowledge Box
Index of Industrial Production (IIP)
IIP is a composite indicator that measures the growth rate of industry groups classified under:
Broad sectors, namely, Mining, Manufacturing and Electricity
Use-based sectors, namely Basic Goods, Capital Goods and Intermediate Goods.
It is currently calculated using 2011-2012 as the base year.
IIP data is compiled and published every month by the Central Statistical Organisation (CSO) under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI).
subject: Economics

questionText: Which one of the following organisations is entrusted with the implementation of the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF)?
options: a) National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development
explanation:
The Government of India created the Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) in the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) in 1995-96, with an initial corpus of Rs.2,000 crore.
Rural Infrastructure Development Fund (RIDF) was created by the government with the aim to build inclusive infrastructure in rural areas.
Under this, NABARD disburses loans as refinancing i.e., it gives loans to banks/governments but not to people directly. Eligible sectors are agriculture and related sectors, social sectors, and rural connectivity etc.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the United Payments Interface (UPI), consider the following statements:
1. It allows users to link more than one bank account in a single smartphone app.
2. It allows money transfers without the Indian Financial System Code (IFSC).
3. Currently, there are no charges levied on transactions done through UPI.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is an instant real-time payment system developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) in conjunction with the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the Indian Banks Association (IBA).
It allows users to link more than one bank account in a single smartphone app and make fund transfers without having to provide an Indian Financial System Code (IFSC) or account number. This is a real-time payment system where funds are credited instantly on a real-time basis. So, statements 1 and 2 are correct.
Currently, there are no charges levied on transactions done through UPI. The UPI programme was launched to promote digital transactions. The National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) had earlier marked the transaction charges to be 50 paise per transaction. However, the Government of India later cancelled these charges to ensure that more and more people use the platform. So, statement 3 is correct.
So, all three of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the National Single Window System (NSWL), consider the following statements:
1. It facilitates regulatory approvals and services related to investments for businesses.
2. It is managed by NITI Aayog in collaboration with the Ministry of Finance.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
The National Single Window System (NSWL) is a one-stop platform for businessmen seeking regulatory approvals and services related to investments. The platform would help businesses identify and apply for required approvals to start or run their businesses. So, statement 1 is correct.
This would bring Transparency, Accountability & Responsiveness to the ecosystem and all information will be available on a single dashboard.
Services that can be availed through the National Single Window System are as follows and not limited to:
Identification of requisite pre-establishment and pre-operation approvals/ registrations for setting up a business unit in India
Application of suggested approval/ registration
Digital repository for storing documents related to approvals/registration for businesses.
Payment of processing fees to issue any approval/ registration
Post application, tracking and status update on applications submitted
Invest India, under the guidance of the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry, manages the National Single Window System project. So, statement 2 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: The term ‘Fabless Industries’ is sometimes seen in the news, with reference to:
explanation:
Fabless industries are companies that design and sell semiconductors (semiconductor chips/silicon wafers) for use in various types of electronic devices, such as in digital cameras, smartphones, smart cars, etc while outsourcing their fabrication.
The term "fabless" (fabrication less) means the company which designs and sells the hardware and semiconductor chips. Instead, it outsources the fabrication to the manufacturing plant called foundry.
Fabless companies are often headquartered in costly, industrialised countries, while semiconductor foundries tend to be headquartered in countries where labour costs are cheaper.
Many of these foundries are located in Taiwan and China, due to the availability of cheap and skilled labour, which keeps production costs low with higher returns on the investment.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC), consider the following statements:
1. It is an aggregator and hosting platform for all existing digital commerce applications.
2. It aims to promote open networks for all aspects of the exchange of goods and services over electronic networks.
3. It will level the playing field for both offline retailers and online e-commerce businesses.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC) is an initiative of the Department of Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce, Government of India.
It aims to promote open networks for all aspects of the exchange of goods and services over digital or electronic networks. It is neither an aggregator application nor a hosting platform and all existing digital commerce applications and platforms can voluntarily choose to adopt and be a part of the ONDC network. So, statement 1 is not correct and statement 2 is correct.
ONDC is to be based on open-sourced methodology, using open specifications and open network protocols independent of any specific platform.
It is being developed as a counter to the current duopoly in the Indian e-commerce market which is largely dictated by Amazon and Walmart-owned Flipkart. It will create a level playing field for online e-commerce websites and offline traders who have been complaining about the unfair trade practices of online e-commerce websites. So, statement 3 is correct.
The platform will also be compliant with the Information Technology Act of 2000 and designed for compliance with the emerging Personal Data Protection Bill.
The platform will allow buyers and sellers to connect and transact with each other online, regardless of what other applications they use. It will also empower merchants and consumers to form a single network to drive innovation and scale, transforming all businesses from retail goods and food to mobility.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the factors of production in India, consider the following statements:
1. A mixed economy consists of both free enterprise and government-controlled socialist economy.
2. In a capitalist economy, the businesses are free to acquire resources and use in the production of any goods.
3. Marxist economics focuses on the role of labour in the development of an economy.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
A mixed economy combines the best features of capitalism and socialism. Thus, mixed economy has some elements of both a free enterprise or capitalist economy as well as a government-controlled socialist economy. The public and private sectors co-exist in mixed economies. So, statement 1 is correct.
The capitalist or free enterprise economy is the oldest form of economy. Earlier economists supported the policy of ‘laissez faire’ that advocated minimum government intervention in economic activities. In a capitalist economy, the government does not control the production decisions of the citizens. Individuals are free to choose any occupation. Freedom of enterprise implies that business firms are free to acquire resources and use them in the production of any good or service. So, statement 2 is correct.
Marxist economics is a school of economic thought based on the work of 19th-century economist and philosopher Karl Marx. The economy focuses on the role of labour in the development of an economy which is critical of the classical approach to wages and productivity developed by Adam Smith. So, statement 3 is correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Competition Commission of India (CCI):
1. It acts as an antitrust watchdog for smaller companies that cannot protect themselves against major enterprises.
2. It has the right to issue warnings to companies that export to India if they are distorting competition in the domestic Indian market.
3. It can only impose non-monetary sanctions on companies for engaging in anti-competitive practices.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
The Competition Commission of India (CCI) is a statutory body of the Government of India responsible for enforcing the Competition Act of 2002. It consists of a Chairperson and 6 Members appointed by the Central Government.
The Commission must eliminate practices having adverse effects on competition, promote and sustain competition, protect the interests of consumers and ensure freedom of trade in the markets of India.
The following are the important functions and powers of CCI:
It is an antitrust watchdog for smaller companies that cannot protect themselves against major enterprises. So, statement 1 is correct.
It provides opinion on competition issues on a reference received from a statutory authority established under any law and undertakes competition advocacy, creates public awareness and imparts training on competition issues.
It has the right to issue warnings to companies that export to India if it believes they are distorting competition in India’s home market. So, statement 2 is correct.
It can impose monetary and non-monetary sanctions for indulging in anti-competitive practices. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Sl.No. Index/Report Released by
1. Global Competitiveness Report : World Trade Organisation
2. Global Economic Prospect Report : International Monetary Fund
3. Energy Transition Index : World Economic Forum
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
explanation:
Global Competitiveness Report: It is a yearly report published by the World Economic Forum. The report assesses the ability of countries to provide high levels of prosperity to their citizens with available resources. So, pair 1 is not correctly matched.
Global Economic Prospect Report: It is a World Bank Group flagship report. This report examines global economic developments and prospects. It focuses on emerging markets and developing economies. It is issued twice a year, in January and June. So, pair 2 is not correctly matched.
Energy Transition Index: This index is released by the World Economic Forum. It tracks nations on the current performance of their energy systems across various aspects. So, pair 3 is correctly matched.
So, only one of the above pairs is correctly matched.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
With reference to the Geographical Indication (GI) Tags, consider the following statements:
1. It is a public property belonging to the producers of the concerned goods.
2. It is governed by the World Trade Organisation (WTO) Agreement on Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS).
3. It can be renewed from time to time for a further period of three years each.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
A Geographical Indication (GI) is a sign used on products that have a specific geographical origin and possess qualities or a reputation that are due to that origin. To function as a GI, a sign must identify a product as originating in a given place.
A geographical indication is a public property belonging to the producers of the concerned goods. It shall not be the subject matter of assignment, transmission, licensing, pledge, mortgage or such other agreement. However, when an authorised user dies, his right devolves on his successor in title. So, statement 1 is correct.
It is used to identify agricultural, natural or manufactured goods. The manufactured goods should be produced or processed or prepared in that territory.
The GI Tag is governed by the World Trade Organisation (WTO) Agreement on Trade-Related Aspects of Intellectual Property Rights (TRIPS). It is also covered as an element of Intellectual Property Rights under the Paris Convention for the Protection of Industrial Property. So, statement 2 is correct.
The registration of a GI tag is valid for a period of 10 years. It can be renewed from time to time for a further period of 10 years each. If a registered GI tag is not renewed, it is liable to be removed from the register. So, statement 3 is not correct.
In India, GI registration is administered by the Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act of 1999.
Indian patents are governed by the Indian Patent Act of 1970.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the “Production Linked Incentive Scheme (PLI)” in India:
1. The total amount of cash subsidy under the scheme is variable and depends upon the annual revenue of the firms.
2. It extends an incentive for incremental sales of goods manufactured in India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme was started in 2020 and is a cornerstone of the Government’s push for achieving an Atmanirbhar Bharat. The objective is to make domestic manufacturing globally competitive and to create global Champions in manufacturing.
Under the PLI Scheme, the total amount of cash subsidy for a particular sector is fixed. So, it is a fund-limited scheme. For example: In 2021 it was decided that the amount of incentive shall be capped on the following basis:
For Air Conditioners - net incremental sale of the eligible product(s) up to 5 times of the cumulative threshold investment in the previous financial year.
It was decided that over performance of any selected applicant shall not be incentivised and the amount of incentive arising out of the underperformance of any selected applicant in any target segment shall not be disbursed to any other applicant. So, statement 1 is not correct.
The scheme shall extend an incentive on incremental sales (over base year) of goods manufactured in India. The selected applicant will also be required to undertake investment, as quoted in their application. The conditions of stipulated Minimum Sales and mandated investment will not be applicable for entities selected for making innovative/organic products. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
Relevance: The Government has been expanding the coverage of PLI and has included the Drone sector as the 14th sector under PLI.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to technical textiles:
1. They are engineered products manufactured using natural as well as man-made fibers.
2. It has been identified as the sunrise industry by the Ministry of Textiles, Government of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
Technical textiles are engineered products with definite functionality. They are manufactured using natural as well as man-made fibres such as Nomex, Kevlar, Spandex, and Twaron that exhibit enhanced functional properties such as higher tenacity, excellent insulation, improved thermal resistance, etc. So, statement 1 is correct.
To position the country as a global leader in Technical Textiles, the National Technical Textiles Mission (NTTM) has been approved with a four-year implementation period from FY 2020-21 to 2023-24.
Technical textile industry has been identified as the sunrise industry by the Ministry of Textiles, Government of India. The National Technical Textile Mission (NTTM) with an outlay of Rs.1,480 Crores aims to place India as one of the leaders in the global technical textile market and enhance the domestic market at the same time. So, statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP):
1. It enables information exchange on a real-time basis amongst all stakeholders in the logistics sector.
2. It is being implemented by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
Unified Logistics Interface Platform (ULIP) is designed to enhance efficiency and reduce the cost of logistics in India by creating a transparent, one-window platform that can provide real-time information to all stakeholders. It was also emphasised that the solution should have the visibility of multi-modal transport, and all the existing systems of various ministries, governing bodies, and private stakeholders should be integrated with the ULIP system. So, statement 1 is correct.
The National Industrial Corridor Development Corporation Limited (NICDC), which works under NITI Aayog, is executing the ULIP platform to reduce costs and improve operational efficiency. So, statement 2 is not correct.
The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) has launched the Unified Logistics Interface Platform’s (ULIP) Hackathon named ‘LogiXtics’. The hackathon will be a competitive event where everyone is invited to showcase their strategic, coding and domain skills on a national-level platform to solve the existing logistics industry issues.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: The term Supply Chain Resilience Initiative (SCRI), sometimes seen in news, is launched by which one of the following groups of counties?
options: d) India, Japan, USA and Australia
explanation:
The Supply Chain Resilience Initiative (SCRI) is a trilateral agreement between trade ministers of India, Japan and Australia, launched in April 2021.
The SCRI aims to create a virtuous cycle of enhancing supply chain resilience infrastructure with a view to eventually attaining strong, sustainable, balanced and inclusive growth in the region.
It seeks to build upon the bilateral frameworks like the ASEAN-Japan Economic Resilience Action Plan and India-Japan Industrial Competitiveness Partnership and attract foreign direct investment in the region.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Self-Reliant India (SRI) Fund:
1. It is a Fund of Funds (FoF) for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs).
2. The Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce & Industry (FICCI) is the anchor investor of the fund.
3. Non-banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) are eligible for funding through the SRI Fund.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
Self-Reliant India (SRI) Fund (Corpus amount Rs. 10,000 crores) is a Fund of Funds (FoF) for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs). It is an Alternative Investment Fund that provides funding to other small funds that invariably provide funding to MSMEs. Thus, SRI does not provide direct funding to MSMEs. So, statement 1 is correct.
The anchor investor of the Fund is the Government of India, through the Union Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises. So, statement 2 is not correct.
This Fund is to be invested in diverse sectors (e.g., traditional MSME, agriculture, pharma, auto, chemicals, etc.). This will ensure a healthy mix of manufacturing, services and agriculture businesses to give impetus to companies in respective segments/ industries. Non-Profit institutions, Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs), financial inclusion sector, micro credit sector, Self Help Groups and other financial intermediaries shall not be eligible for consideration. So, statement 3 is not correct.
So, only one of the above statements is correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following:
1. Property
2. Technology
3. Rent
4. Insurance
5. Inventory costs
In India, spending on how many of the above comes under Capital Expenditure (Capex)?
explanation:
Capital Expenditures (Capex):
Capex are funds used by a company to acquire, upgrade, and maintain physical assets such as property, plants, buildings, technology, or equipment. So, points 1 and 2 are correct.
Capex is often used to undertake new projects or investments by a company. Making capital expenditures on fixed assets can include repairing a roof (if the useful life of the roof is extended), purchasing a piece of equipment, or building a new factory.
This type of financial outlay is made by companies to increase the scope of their operations or add some future economic benefit to the operation.
Operating Expense (Opex):
An operating expense is an expense a business incurs through its normal business operations.
It includes rent, equipment, inventory costs, marketing, payroll, insurance, step costs, and funds allocated for research and development. So, points 3, 4 and 5 are not correct.
So, in India, spending on only two of the above comes under Capital Expenditure (Capex).
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the ‘Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Electric Vehicles in India-II (FAME India-II) Scheme’:
1. It provides incentives for both plug-in hybrid vehicles and battery electric vehicles.
2. It provides for the establishment of charging infrastructure in metros and cities of hilly States.
3. Currently, no Goods and Service Tax (GST) is levied on charging stations for electric vehicles.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
Phase-II of Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Electric Vehicles (FAME) India Scheme is being implemented for a period of 3 years from 01st April 2019 with total budgetary support of Rs. 10,000 crores. It has recently been extended by two years and will now be in force till March 31, 2024.
The Department of Heavy Industry (DHI) is administering the FAME India Scheme for the promotion of the adoption of electric/hybrid vehicles (Evs) in India since 01st April 2015.
FAME-II Scheme mainly focuses on supporting the electrification of public and shared transportation. The scheme covers Hybrid & Electric technologies like Mild Hybrid, Strong Hybrid, and Plug-in Hybrid & Battery Electric Vehicles. So, Statement 1 is correct.
The scheme proposes for establishing charging infrastructure and for this about 2700 charging stations will be established in metros, other million-plus cities, smart cities and cities of Hilly states across the country. The objective is to create at least one charging station in a grid of 3 km x 3 km. Charging stations will also be established on major highways connecting major city clusters. So, statement 2 is correct.
Currently the Goods and Service Tax (GST) rate on all Electric Vehicles has been reduced from 12% to 5% and of charger or charging stations for EVs from 18% to 5%. It is aimed at encouraging the industry to pass on more cost benefits to the users. So, Statement 3 is not correct.
So, only two of the above statements are correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer
subject: Economics

questionText: Which one of the following statements best describes the ‘Kuznets curve hypothesis’?
options: a) It explains a trade-off between the rate of inflation and the level of employment in an economy.
b) It demonstrates the correlation between tax rates and the amount of tax revenue collected by governments.
c) It is a graphical representation of distribution of income or wealth within a population.
d) It proposes that economic growth initially causes a rise in inequality, followed by a subsequent decline.
explanation:
Kuznets curve demonstrates the hypothesis that economic growth initially leads to greater inequality, followed later by the reduction of inequality. The idea was first proposed by American economist Simon Kuznets.
The Phillips curve is an economic theory that inflation and unemployment have a stable and inverse relationship. Developed by William Phillips, it claims that with economic growth comes inflation, which in turn should lead to more jobs and less unemployment.
The Laffer Curve is based on a theory by supply-side economist Arthur Laffer. Created in 1974, it visually shows the relationship between tax rates and the amount of tax revenue collected by governments. The curve is often used to illustrate the argument that cutting tax rates can result in increased total tax revenue.
Lorenz’s Curve is a graphical representation of the distribution of income or wealth within a population. It uses the information expressed in a cumulative manner to indicate the degree of inequality.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: Which one of the following is the primary objective of the ‘e-AMRIT’ portal, launched by the Government of India?
options: a) To track the progress made on the implementation of Jal Jeevan Mission
b) To increase investments in the field of traditional medicines
c) To ensure sustainable extraction of groundwater for irrigation purposes
d) To create awareness about electric mobility
explanation:
The e-AMRIT (Accelerated e-Mobility Revolution for India's Transportation) portal aims to create awareness about electric mobility in India. The portal aims to serve as a ‘one-stop site’ to provide all the information related to the adoption of electric vehicles in India. e-AMRIT is the result of a joint initiative between NITI Aayog and the UK Government.
By providing access to information, the e-AMRIT portal assists the users to:
Switch to electric vehicles by providing insights about electric vehicle technologies, types of electric vehicles, insurance options, and financing options.
Set up the electric vehicle or associated enterprise by providing insights on the key initiatives of central and state governments.
Assess the benefits of electric vehicles with uniquely designed tools to determine the users' savings with electric vehicles compared to petrol/diesel vehicles.
Get all the information on the Indian electric vehicle market and industry and the key developments driving the e-mobility ecosystem forward.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following:
1. Cultivable Biological Resources
2. Machinery and equipment
3. Intellectual Property Product
How many of the above components are included in the Gross Fixed Capital Formation?
explanation:
Gross Fixed Capital Formation (GFCF) is the net amount of fixed capital accumulation which is called net investment. It measures the increase in the capital stock less the disposal of fixed assets. Thus, Gross fixed capital formation is not a measure of total investment as only the value of net additions to fixed assets.
GFCF comprises four components, namely Dwellings, other buildings and structures (DOBS), Machinery and Equipment (ME), Cultivable Biological Resources (CBR) and Intellectual Property Product (IPP). So, points 1, 2 and 3 are correct.
Fixed Assets are those which are used repeatedly or continuously in production processes for more than one year. Fixed assets include not only structures, machinery and equipment but also cultivable biological resources such as trees, plants, and animals that are used repeatedly or continuously to produce other products such as fruits and dairy products. They also include intellectual property products such as software, databases and products of artistic originals used in production.
So, all three of the above components are included in the Gross Fixed Capital Formation.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Hybrid Annuity Model (HAM):
1. It is a mix of Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) and Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) models.
2. Under HAM, the cost of the project is borne by the government and the private companies equally.
3. Under HAM, revenue collection is the responsibility of the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI).
Which of the statements given above are correct?
explanation:
In India, the new HAM is a mix of Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) and Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) models. The advantage of HAM is that it gives enough liquidity to the developer and the financial risk is shared by the government. So, statement 1 is correct.
While the private partner continues to bear the construction and maintenance risks as in the case of the BOT (toll) model, he is required only to partly bear the financing risk. There is no toll right for the developer. Under HAM, revenue collection is the responsibility of the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI). So, statement 3 is correct.
In the prescribed structure, the government allocates 40% of the project cost during the initial five years through annual fixed payments (annuity). The subsequent payment is contingent upon the assets' value and the developer's performance, constituting a hybrid annuity where the initial 40% is disbursed in five equal instalments, while the remaining 60% is variable and determined post-project completion. So, statement 2 is not correct.
Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
The Reserve Bank of India’s recent directives relating to ‘New Tokenisation norms’ require the merchants to:
1. use unique tokens instead of debit card information used at the time of online sale
2. not store actual card details used during the processing of the transactions
3. facilitate payments through contactless cards transactions
Which of the statements given above are correct?
explanation:
Tokenization refers to the replacement of actual card details with a unique alternate code called the ‘token’. This token would be unique for a combination of card, token requester, (i.e. the entity which accepts requests from the customer for tokenization of a card and passes it on to the card network to issue a corresponding token), and the device. A tokenized card transaction is considered safer as the actual card details are not shared with the merchant during transaction processing.
As per the guidelines of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI), no online retailers or payment gateways will be permitted to save any credit card or debit card information of the customers on their digital platform. Thus, it is mandatory for all credit and debit card data used in online, point-of-sale, and in-app transactions to be replaced with unique tokens. So, point 1 is correct.
A tokenized card transaction is considered safer as the actual card details are not shared with the merchant during transaction processing. Actual card data, tokens and other relevant details are stored in a secure mode by the authorised card networks. So, point 2 is correct.
Tokenization has been allowed through mobile phones and/or tablets for all use cases/channels e.g., contactless card transactions, payments through QR codes, apps etc.). So, point 3 is correct.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
Relevance: Recently, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has made tokenisation mandatory for all credit and debit cards used in online, point-of-sale, and in-app transactions.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to Venture Capitalists (VC):
1. They are individual investors who invest in start-up businesses in exchange for equity.
2. They do not get involved in the decision-making of the invested companies.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
Venture capital financing is commonly associated with the provision of equity investment for a while in small/medium businesses with high growth potential and high reward but which could entail high risk.
Venture capitalists are firms or individual investors and banking institutions with a large base, who take a great deal of risk to invest in newly started ventures or startups with promising growth for the future. They tap such small businesses and can have an eye on them through mentorship, and guidance for growth. They expect equity stake and or profit ratios once the business gets established as returns. So, statement 1 is correct.
Venture Capital investments are risky as they are illiquid, but are capable of giving impressive returns if invested in the right venture. The returns to the venture capitalists depend upon the growth of the company. Venture capitalists have the power to influence major decisions of the companies they are investing in as it is their money at stake. So, statement 2 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: Which one of the following grants infrastructure status to projects and sectors in India?
options: b) Financial Stability and Development Council
d) Department of Economic Affairs, Union Ministry of Finance
explanation:
The Department of Economic Affairs (DAE), Union Ministry of Finance grants the Infrastructure Status to the projects/sectors in India.
The Ministry maintains a Harmonized Master List of Infrastructure Sub-sectors which includes sectors, namely Transport and Logistics, Energy, Water, Sanitation, Communication, Social and Commercial Infrastructure, etc.
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: The terms ‘SWEEKAR’ and ‘SWAGAT’ recently seen in news is related to:
options: b) Welfare programme for Tribal Community
explanation:
India’s first indigenously developed payment ecosystem for transport consists of -
NCMC:
These are bank issued cards on Debit/Credit/Prepaid card product Rupay platform.
The customer may use this single card for payments across all segments including metro, bus, suburban railways, toll, parking, smart city and retail.
SWEEKAR (Swachalit Kiraya): An Automatic Fare Collection (AFC) System
AFC System (gates, readers/validators, backend infrastructure etc.) is the core of any transit operator to automate the fare collection process.
SWAGAT (Swachalit Gate): a gate and card-reader system.
The Gate & Reader prototype has been made by BEL. This is the first gate and the reader which has been manufactured by an Indian company.
This is also the first indigenous payment reader which has been certified as per International standards. India joins the very few elite nations who have indigenous capacity on gate and reader production.
Therefore, Option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements regarding Nobel Prize in Economics:
1. It was awarded to Claudia Goldin for having advanced our understanding of women’s labour market outcomes.
2. The participation of married women increased with the transition from an agrarian to an industrial society in the early nineteenth century.
3. Female participation in the labour market depicts an inverse U-shaped curve.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
The Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences has decided to award the Sveriges Riksbank Prize in Economic Sciences in Memory of Alfred Nobel 2023 to Claudia Goldin “for having advanced our understanding of women’s labour market outcomes”. Hence, statement 1 is correct.
Goldin showed that female participation in the labour market did not have an upward trend over this entire period, but instead formed a U-shaped curve. The participation of married women decreased with the transition from an agrarian to an industrial society in the early nineteenth century, but then started to increase with the growth of the service sector in the early twentieth century. Goldin explained this pattern as the result of structural change and evolving social norms regarding women’s responsibilities for home and family.
It did not depicted Inverted U-shape curve. Hence, Statement 2 and Statement 3 are incorrect.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statement regarding ‘Harit Sagar’ guidelines:
1. They will be implemented by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
2. The green belt area shall be increased by more than 20 percent by 2030 and 33 percent by 2047 of the port area.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
To reduce carbon intensity and to develop an environment friendly ecosystem at Major Ports with participation of all stakeholders including Terminal Operators, Logistics Service Providers, Partner Government Agencies, Shipping Lines etc. Harit Dhara ’ Green Port Guidelines 2023 will be implemented by the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways. Hence, Statement 1 is incorrect.
Ports shall increase the green area cover to capture the fugitive emissions and attenuate the noise generated in the Port surroundings by carrying out effective plantation, developing landscapes etc. The green belt area shall be increased by more than 20 percent by 2030 and 33 percent by 2047 of the port area. Green belt will help in: Supporting biological diversity, Retaining soil moisture. Erosion control and coastal protection, Recharging ground water and acting as Carbon Sink. Hence, Statement 2 is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: Recently, seen in the news ‘Bharat NCAP’ is related to?
explanation:
Bharat NCAP is a “New Car Safety Assessment program” which proposes a mechanism of awarding ‘Star Ratings’ to automobiles based upon their performance in crash tests. The Indian New Car Assessment Program, more commonly referred to as BNCAP, is India’s independent voice on vehicle safety. We crash test cars and conduct performance assessments on safety features and technologies then publish a simple star rating to indicate relative safety performance.
Bharat New Car Assessment Program will be applicable to the vehicles of category M1 with GVW up to 3500 kg.
The program is voluntary in nature & above the regulatory requirements.
Therefore, Option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following minerals:
1. Lithium
2. Beryllium
3. Niobium
4. Titanium
5. Tantalum
6. Zirconium
How many of the above mentioned minerals have been removed from the Limited list which were reserved for state agencies by Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Amendment Act, 2023?
explanation:
The Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Amendment Act, 2023, amends the Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, 1957, with the aim of strengthening the exploration and extraction of critical minerals essential for India's economic development and national security. The key amendments brought about by the bill include:
Removal of 6 minerals from the list of 12 atomic minerals limited to exploration by State agencies (i.e., lithium, beryllium, niobium, titanium, tantalum and zirconium). This opens up opportunities for private sector involvement in their exploration and mining. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.
Introduction of Exploration Licences (EL) to attract foreign direct investment (FDI) and engage junior mining companies in exploring deep-seated and critical minerals like gold, platinum, rare earth elements, etc. While 100% FDI is allowed in mining, there has been limited investment in these sectors. The EL, granted through auction, enables licensees to conduct reconnaissance and prospecting for specified minerals
Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText: The ‘Harrod-Domar Model’ is related to:
options: a) Model to disinvest from PSUs in a profitable manner.
b) Economic growth model emphasising on Investment and Saving.
c) Model to smoothen the Insolvency resolution process.
d) Theory of comparative advantage in international trade.
explanation:
The Harrod-Domar models of economic growth are based on the experiences of advanced capitalist economies to analyse the requirements of steady growth in such an economy. The Harrod-Domar economic growth model stresses the importance of savings and investment as key determinants of growth. The model emphases on the dual character of investment:
It creates income which is regarded as the ‘demand effect’.
It augments the productive capacity of the economy by increasing its capital stock which is regarded as the ‘supply effect’ of investment.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following pairs with respect to India’s Partnership:
Sr No. Initiative Partnering Country
1. i-CET USA
2. Trade and Technology Council Australia
3. Cyber and Cyber – Enabled Critical Technology European Union
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
explanation:
In May 2022, India and US announced the U.S.-India initiative on Critical and Emerging Technology (iCET) to elevate and expand strategic technology partnership and defence industrial cooperation between the governments, businesses, and academic institutions of two countries. The initiative is led by Nationals Security Advisor’s (NSA) of both the countries and has six priority sectors – defence, space, quantum, artificial intelligence, semiconductors and telecommunications. Hence, pair 1 is correctly matched.
The India-EU Trade and Technology Council (TTC) was established in May 2023. Under the TTC three working groups have been set up which are focused on enhancing cooperation in the areas of strategic technologies, digital governance and digital connectivity, green and clean energy technologies and trade, investment and resilient value chains. Hence, pair 2 is not correctly matched.
India and Australia signed the Framework Arrangement on Cyber and Cyber – Enabled Critical Technology Cooperation in June 2020. In 2023, India and Japan have also entered into a Memorandum of Cooperation (MoC) on semiconductor supply chain partnership. Hence, pair 3 is not correctly matched.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements:
1. Pharmacy Council of India (PCI), is a statutory body under the Ministry of Health and family welfare.
2. The Indian pharma industry ranks 3rd globally in pharmaceutical production by value.
3. Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO), is responsible for approval of Drugs, Conduct of Clinical Trials.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
The Pharmacy education and profession in India upto graduate level is regulated by the PCI, a statutory body governed by the provisions of the Pharmacy Act, 1948 passed by the Parliament. Hence, statement 1 is correct.
The Indian pharma industry ranks 3rd globally in pharmaceutical production by volume and 14th globally by value. Hence, Statement 2 is incorrect.
The Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO) under the Directorate General of Health Services,Ministry of Health & Family Welfare,Government of India is the National Regulatory Authority (NRA) of India. The Drugs & Cosmetics Act,1940 and rules 1945 have entrusted various responsibilities to central & state regulators for regulation of drugs & cosmetics. Under the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, CDSCO is responsible for approval of Drugs, Conduct of Clinical Trials, laying down the standards for Drugs, control over the quality of imported Drugs. Hence, statement 3 is correct.
Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statement regarding Indian Semiconductor Mission (ISM):
1. It operates under the guidance of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
2. Union Minister (MeitY) will be the chairperson of the ISM Committee.
3. Cabinet has approved financial outlay of Rs 760 crore for this mission.
How many of the above statements are correct?
explanation:
India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) is a specialised and independent Business Division within the Digital India Corporation that aims to build a vibrant semiconductor and display ecosystem to enable India’s emergence as a global hub for electronics manufacturing and design. The India Semiconductor Mission is driven by the Indian government in collaboration with various industry associations, research organisations, and educational institutions. It operates under the guidance of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) and other relevant government bodies. Hence, Statement 1 is correct.
Secretary to MeitY is the chairperson of ISM Committee. Hence, Statement 2 is incorrect.
The government has approved the Semicon India programme with a total outlay of Rs 76,000 crore for the development of semiconductor and display manufacturing ecosystem in the country. The programme has further been modified in view of the aggressive incentives offered by countries already having an established semiconductor ecosystem and limited number of companies owning the advanced node technologies. Hence, statement 3 is incorrect.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics

questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference to National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP):
1. It is a group of social and economic infrastructure projects that aims to achieve the $5 trillion economy by 2024-25.
2. Entire cost of the NIP is shared equally between the Central and the concerned State Governments.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
explanation:
The National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) is a group of social and economic infrastructure projects in India over a period of five years with an initial sanctioned amount of ₹102 lakh crore to achieve the $5 Trillion economy by 2024-25. NIP will cover the period from 2019-20 to 2024-25. So, statement 1 is correct.
As per the NIP, Central Government (39 per cent) and State Government (39 per cent) are expected to have an equal share of funding of the projects followed by the private sector (22 per cent). Since the private sector would absorb 22 percent of the project cost, the state and the central governments will not bear the entire cost of the project. So, statement 2 is not correct.
Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.
subject: Economics
